reasoning: Real interest rates broadly follow a random walk in the short to medium term. Although there are macroeconomic cases for both higher real rates (e.g., persistent deficits and term premium rebuilding) and lower real rates (e.g., monetary easing and falling inflation), there is no strong predictable trend that skews the probability away from roughly 50% over a typical forecasting horizon. Absent a specific list of resolution dates or obvious expected macro directional bias for the exact period, an estimate of 50% best captures this symmetric uncertainty.
